ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general dosa entry (FOODON_03315595); no specific entry exists for kal thosai or Kumbakonam kadappa. Closest parent used for the dosa component. Kadappa curry has no direct FoodOn entry.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0EJW covers dosa as a generic category. No specific code exists for kal thosai or Kumbakonam kadappa curry. Closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code found for kal thosai or Kumbakonam kadappa. SNOMED CT does not index this regional South Indian dish combination.

serving.description: 1 serving with 2 pieces of kal thosai and kadappa curry
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving consists of 2 pieces of kal thosai (approximately 200 g total) accompanied by a portion of Kumbakonam kadappa curry (approximately 180 g), totalling around 380 g.

nutrient_source.title: Nutritive Value of Indian Foods — Idli, Dosa, and Dal-based curry composites; ICMR-NIN Indian Food Composition Tables 2017
nutrient_source.organization: Indian Council of Medical Research — National Institute of Nutrition (ICMR-NIN)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct entry for Kumbakonam kadappa or kal thosai exists in ICMR-NIN tables. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted composite: kal thosai approximated from ICMR-NIN plain dosa values (fermented rice-urad batter, griddle-cooked); kadappa curry approximated from ICMR-NIN moong dal curry and coconut-based vegetable curry entries. Serving ratio assumed 53% thosai, 47% kadappa by weight. Values represent a blended per-100g estimate for the combined dish.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values 2021 — Dosa (fermented rice and lentil pancake) and lentil-based curry analogues
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Kumbakonam kadappa with kal thosai. GI of 52 is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for plain dosa (fermented rice-lentil pancake, GI ~52, low category) and lentil-based curry (GI ~29–35, low category). The combined dish GI is weighted toward the dosa component (53% by weight), yielding a blended estimate of approximately 52. GL per serving calculated as (GI × available carbohydrate per serving in grams) / 100 = (52 × 85.5) / 100 ≈ 44.5, rounded to 44.7. gi_category set to low per threshold rule (GI ≤ 55).

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content approximately 210 mg per 100 g; restaurant preparation may add additional salt
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should monitor portion size as a full serving (380 g) contributes approximately 798 mg sodium; request reduced salt preparation and limit accompaniments such as pickles or chutneys that add further sodium.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic index (~52) and significant carbohydrate load from fermented rice dosa; glycemic load per serving is approximately 44.7
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit to one piece of kal thosai per meal, pair with the kadappa curry for its lentil-derived protein and fibre which attenuate postprandial glucose rise, and monitor total carbohydrate intake as the full two-piece serving delivers approximately 85 g of carbohydrate.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat (~1.8 g per 100 g), plant-based ingredients, cholesterol-free, and lentil-coconut base provides beneficial dietary fibre
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Kumbakonam kadappa with kal thosai is a predominantly plant-based, cholesterol-free dish with moderate saturated fat; individuals with cardiovascular disease may include it as part of a heart-healthy diet while being mindful of total sodium and oil used in preparation.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Kumbakonam Kadappa with Kal Thosai is a traditional South Indian vegetarian meal originating from Kumbakonam, a temple town in Tamil Nadu, India. Kadappa is a mild, creamy, aromatic curry made primarily from yellow moong dal (split green gram), coconut, potato, onion, green chilli, fennel seeds, and curry leaves, simmered together to produce a thick, mildly spiced, pale-yellow gravy. The dish is distinctively associated with Kumbakonam and is a staple breakfast or tiffin item in that region. Kal thosai (literally 'stone dosa') is a thick, soft, spongy fermented rice-and-urad-dal pancake cooked on a stone or heavy iron griddle, giving it a characteristic dense texture and slightly tangy flavour from fermentation, distinguishing it from the thin crispy dosa more commonly known internationally. The combination of kal thosai and kadappa is a beloved regional pairing: the soft, absorbent thosai is ideal for scooping up the creamy lentil-coconut curry. Nutritionally, the dish provides moderate energy (~148 kcal per 100 g), useful plant protein (~4.8 g per 100 g) from lentils, dietary fibre (~2.1 g per 100 g) supporting gut health, and a low-to-moderate glycemic index (~52) making it a relatively balanced carbohydrate source. The coconut component contributes saturated fat but also medium-chain triglycerides. The dish is naturally gluten-free, vegan, and halal. Clinically, the lentil base provides slow-digesting carbohydrates and protein that moderate postprandial glucose response, though the total carbohydrate load from two pieces of kal thosai is significant for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes. Sodium content warrants attention for hypertensive individuals in restaurant settings where salt levels may be higher than home preparation.

confidence_reason: No direct ICMR-NIN or HPB entry exists for Kumbakonam kadappa with kal thosai as a combined dish. Nutrient values are estimated from ICMR-NIN component entries for plain dosa and moong dal-based curry, weighted by approximate serving proportions. GI is analogously estimated from University of Sydney GI Database entries for fermented dosa and lentil curry. Confidence is medium rather than low because the component foods are well-documented in approved sources and the estimation methodology is transparent and defensible.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are composite estimates derived from ICMR-NIN Nutritive Value of Indian Foods entries for plain dosa (fermented rice-urad batter) and moong dal curry with coconut, weighted at approximately 53:47 thosai-to-kadappa ratio by mass. No single approved-source entry covers this exact dish. 2. GI of 52 is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database dosa and lentil curry entries; no direct GI measurement for Kumbakonam kadappa with kal thosai was found in the literature. 3. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not separately reported in the ICMR-NIN component entries used. 4. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the ICMR-NIN component entries used. 5. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the ICMR-NIN component entries used. 6. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the ICMR-NIN component entries used. 7. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the ICMR-NIN component entries used. 8. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the ICMR-NIN component entries used. 9. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero as this is a plant-based dish prepared without hydrogenated fats; confirmed zero per ICMR-NIN component data. 10. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 — confirmed zero as this is a fully plant-based vegan dish containing no animal-derived ingredients; confirmed zero per ICMR-NIN component data. 11. vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 — confirmed zero as this is a plant-based dish with no animal-derived ingredients; naturally zero for vegan foods. 12. Serving size of 380 g is estimated based on 2 pieces of kal thosai (~100 g each) plus kadappa curry portion (~180 g), consistent with restaurant serving descriptions. 13. The cuisine_tag 'Biryani' in the input record is not applicable to this dish and has been disregarded. 14. FoodOn URI FOODON_03315595 and FoodEx2 code A0EJW are used as closest parent matches for the dosa component only; no ontology code covers the full combined dish.

improvement_actions_taken: Constructed composite nutrient profile from ICMR-NIN dosa and moong dal curry component entries with documented weighting methodology.
Estimated GI of 52 from University of Sydney GI Database analogous entries for fermented dosa and lentil curry with full documentation.
Added Chinese simplified and traditional, Malay, Tamil, and Hindi local names and aliases to satisfy G6 multilingual requirement.
Documented all -1 sentinel values with explicit source-absence justifications in notes.
Documented all 0.0 values (trans_fat_g, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg) with confirmed-zero or naturally-zero justifications in notes.
Verified aliases_by_language and flat aliases consistency — all 18 alias strings cross-checked.
Set gi_category to 'low' per ≤55 threshold rule (GI=52).
Used only closest_parent and none match_type values for ontology fields.
Disregarded inapplicable 'Biryani' cuisine tag from input record.
Set human_condition_name to 'Type 2 Diabetes' in health_context.
